title: Odd Lots: How to Run One of London’s Most Popular Pubs (Podcast)
summary: As our listeners know, restaurants are great microcosms for macro-economic trends. They sit at the intersection of everything from consumer confidence to commodity costs to the labor market. So on our recent visit to London, we wanted to learn about the business of pubs. According to the British Beer and Pub Association, approximately two pubs a day have closed in England during the first quarter of 2026. Could pubs tell us something about larger trends in the British economy? And when it comes

title: Oil Holds Decline as Traders See Progress Toward Iranian Deal
summary: Oil held a drop on signs that negotiations to extend a US-Iranian ceasefire and reopen the Strait of Hormuz were making progress.

title: Gold Holds Gain as Iran Deal Progress Eases Inflation Concerns
summary: Gold held gains on signs the US and Iran were making progress on reaching a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and restore oil flows, easing inflation fears.

title: Asian Stocks Set to Extend Gains on Iran Optimism: Markets Wrap
summary: Asian stocks were poised to extend gains as optimism over a potential US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz lifted risk appetite and sent oil prices lower.

title: A Scorching Asian Summer Will Add to Risk of Surging Gas Prices
summary: As the global gas market grapples with the Strait of Hormuz being all-but closed for nearly three months, traders are fixated on two wildcards: China and the weather.

title: Strategists Warn of High Yields Even If Iran War Ends
summary: For all the hand-wringing over war-related inflation fears, there are signs that other drivers are having as much a bearing on longer-term borrowing costs.

title: Treasury Curve Flashes Higher-for-Longer Warning Under Warsh
summary: A key Treasury yield gap has shrunk to its tightest level in a year as traders ramp up bets the Federal Reserve may keep interest rates higher for longer under new chairman Kevin Warsh.

title: Codelco Union Threatens Protests as Misreporting Fallout Spreads
summary: One of Codelco’s biggest unions vowed to fight any attempt to claw back bonuses as fallout from the overstatement of copper output at the Chilean state miner continues to spread.

title: Korea Short-Term Debt Pricing Too Many BOK Hikes, NH Amundi Says
summary: South Korea’s bond market is pricing in an excessive number of central bank rate hikes, creating buying opportunities in short-term debt, according to NH Amundi Asset Management Co.

title: Aegea Investors, Equatorial Bid in One of Brazil’s Biggest Deals This Year
summary: Two groups submitted bids to become the strategic investor in Copasa, advancing the Minas Gerais state government’s plan to privatize one of Brazil’s largest water and sewage utilities, according to people familiar to the matter.

title: Iran Deal Hopes Boost Emerging Market Assets as Oil Slides
summary: Emerging-market stocks and currencies rose Monday as signs that the US and Iran are nearing a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz pushed oil prices lower and boosted risk appetite.

title: Oil Slides as US Touts Progress on Deal Toward Reopening Hormuz
summary: West Texas Intermediate crude briefly fell below $90 a barrel for the first time in almost three weeks amid signs the US and Iran were progressing toward an agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Gold Gains as Prospects of Iran Deal Temper Inflation Concerns
summary: Gold advanced after officials signaled the US was nearing a deal with Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and restore oil flows, easing inflation fears.

title: Hungary Sees May 28 Deal With EU on Frozen Funds, PM Magyar Says
summary: Hungarian Prime Minister Peter Magyar said he’ll sign a political accord with the head of the European Union’s executive on May 28 on the release of the bloc’s frozen funds.

title: Cox Group Took 20% Bridge Loan for Iberdrola Mexico Deal
summary: Spanish utility Cox ABG Group SA took a bridge loan to help fund a $4.2 billion asset acquisition in Mexico and placed shares as collateral.

title: Vigilant ECB Hasn’t Yet Seen Second-Round Effects, Villeroy Says
summary: A spike in energy costs hasn’t yet turned into second-round effects in other parts of the euro-area economy, according to outgoing European Central Bank Governing Council member Francois Villeroy de Galhau.

title: Ghana to Start Buying 30% of Large Gold Mines’ Ouput From June
summary: Ghana’s central bank said it plans to increase gold purchases from the West African nation’s large-scale producers to 30% of their output from 20%, starting June 1.

title: US Stock Futures Gain as Crude Oil Drops on Iran: Markets Wrap
summary: Global stocks rose to record highs as crude oil fell after officials signaled the US was nearing a deal with Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and restore oil flows. The dollar weakened.

title: European Stocks Advance on Iran Deal Hopes; Delivery Hero Jumps
summary: European shares advanced as oil declined, with the US and Iran inching toward a deal to end the nearly-three-month war. Italian stocks notched a fresh record.

title: Czech Premier Urges Interest Rate Cut as Inflation Risks Grow
summary: The Czech Republic’s prime minister urged the central bank to cut interest rates, doubling down on his push for lower borrowing costs even as the country faces growing inflation risks from energy prices.

title: Top Bauxite Producer Guinea to Unveil Export Controls in June
summary: Guinea, the world’s biggest bauxite producer, plans to announce reforms next month to control exports of the ore used in making aluminum as it bids to bolster prices.

title: Nigeria’s First Quarter GDP Eases on Slowdown in Oil Sector
summary: Nigeria’s economy grew at a slower-than-expected pace in the first quarter as growth in both its oil and non-oil sectors eased.

title: Top Africa Bourse to Tighten Algo-Trading, Market-Access Rules
summary: Africa’s biggest stock-exchange operator is working to tighten oversight of how brokers and trading firms access the bourse to lessen the risk of rogue algorithms and trading errors that could disrupt the market.

title: Iran War Squeezes India’s Gas Power Supply as Demand Hits Record
summary: India’s gas power generation has plunged to the lowest level in at least six years, with the Iran war hitting fuel shipments, straining electricity supplies at a time when a scorching summer is pushing demand to record highs.

title: Italian Stocks Hit First Record in 26 Years Led by Energy, Chips
summary: Italy’s benchmark equity index rose past its all-time closing high set in 2000, with a recent rally in energy and chip stocks supercharging it to record levels.

title: India Raises Diesel, Gasoline Prices for Fourth Time in May
summary: India’s state-run fuel retailers raised gasoline and diesel prices for the fourth time in 10 days in response to disruptions in global energy markets, a move that risks exacerbating inflationary risks in the economy.

title: Zepto Said to Plan June Public Filing for $1 Billion India IPO
summary: Rapid-commerce firm Zepto Ltd. is preparing to publicly file in the first half of June for an initial public offering that may raise up to $1 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Iran Says US Deal Not Imminent | The Pulse 5/25/2026
summary:
Iran has denied that a deal to end the war with the US is imminent, after US officials said they were closing in on a deal. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said an agreement could come as soon as Monday, but Iran’s Foreign Ministry Spokesman Esmail Baghaei said “It is true that a consensus was reached on many of the topics discussed, but no one can claim that the signing of an agreement is imminent.”

Turkish police entered the headquarters of the main opposition CHP party following a court decision last week, that removed Ozgur Ozel as its leader. The move deepens a political crisis that saw a market selloff last week.

And Huawei says it's made a breakthrough in making advanced semiconductors without cutting-edge equipment, potentially reducing the gap with industry leader TSMC.

Today's guests: Marco Valli, UniCredit, Chief European Economist & Alex Karnal, Braidwell, Co-Founder and CIO. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Italy Tightens Grip on Payments Company Nexi With Stake Increase
summary: Italy’s state lender Cassa Depositi e Prestiti SpA is set to increase its stake in Nexi SpA, tightening its grip on the payment company considered strategic by Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni’s government.

title: SoftBank Taps Retail Market Again With $1.6 Billion Yen Bond
summary: SoftBank Group Corp. plans to raise ¥260 billion ($1.6 billion) through another sale of subordinated bonds aimed mainly at individual investors, about two months after a similar retail offering.
